qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stored XSS in chat message rendering executes attacker HTML in recipient sessions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an authenticated chat-message HTML injection rendered with jQuery .html(), while the advisory is a default remember-me cookie encryption key and missing rotation flaw in the authentication configuration.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's remember-me cookie encryption key defaulting and non-rotation root cause; the only final finding is a chat stored XSS in a different code path.
evidence: The advisory describes a hard-coded remember-me encryption key in openmeetings.properties and missing automatic rotation, enabling credential recovery from a stolen cookie when administrators leave the default key unchanged.

Upstream 8.1.0 ships remember.me.encryption.key=27574200-a56f-410a-b2c9-3aa3b4b9389a and remember.me.encryption.salt=abrakadabra in openmeetings-web/src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/classes/openmeetings.properties, and Application injects those values into OmAuthenticationStrategy for remember-me cookie cryptography.

Upstream 9.0.0 removes the static key and salt properties, adds remember.me.rotation.days, and initializes remember-me via initRememberMe(), which regenerates a persisted key when missing or outdated.

The session's only qualifying finding traces attacker-controlled chat HTML through ChatForm and ChatWebSocketHelper into chat.js .html(), which is a stored XSS root cause unrelated to remember-me cookie key management.
